title: Velvety Nostalgic Venezuelan Shrimp Empanada
description: A winey, velvety, nostalgic Venezuelan dish featuring shrimp stock, render-fat, and a Jain-friendly twist. This empanada is perfect for a dinner party or a cozy night in.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink, about 2 minutes per side.
Add the shrimp stock, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2 minutes.
Meanwhile, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the shrimp mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Bake the empanadas on a baking sheet for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, prepare the wine sauce by reducing 1 cup of white wine in a small saucepan over medium heat until almost syrupy.
When the empanadas are done, brush them with a little bit of the wine sauce and sprinkle with chopped fresh cilantro.
